hero.title: May 26 Spain Trip
hero.heading: Barcelona, Tossa de Mar, Granada, and Seville in one shared trip flow.
hero.intro: A continuous Spain trip from Barcelona through Tossa de Mar, Granada, and Seville, updated to remove Joan Miró, Blanes or Sitges, and Tibidabo while keeping the fixed Sagrada Família visit and a full Tossa de Mar day.
snapshot: Barcelona hotel: Renaissance Barcelona Hotel, Pau Claris 122
Fixed entry: Sagrada Família on May 19 at 1:30 PM
Day trip swap: Tossa de Mar replaces Blanes / Sitges

overview.heading: Trip Shape
overview.body: This source combines five Barcelona days, a full Tossa de Mar excursion, two Granada days, and the final Seville stretch into one continuous May itinerary.

overview.heading: Barcelona Hotel Note
overview.body: The Barcelona base is Renaissance Barcelona Hotel on Pau Claris in Eixample. Keep the itinerary as-is, but use the hotel-based map links for arrival, Gothic Quarter walks, Gaudi/Eixample days, Tossa de Mar, and the May 21 airport transfer.

overview.heading: Booking Priorities
overview.body: Secure the Alhambra, Sagrada Família, Real Alcázar, Seville Cathedral, Casa Batlló, Casa Milà, Park Güell, and Picasso Museum first, then layer restaurant reservations around those fixed anchors.

destinations.name: Renaissance Barcelona Hotel
destinations.summary: Barcelona hotel base on Pau Claris in Eixample, still walkable to the Gothic Quarter and especially convenient for Passeig de Gracia, Casa Batllo, and Casa Mila.

destinations.name: Barcelona-El Prat Airport
destinations.summary: Barcelona airport anchor for the arrival transfer to the Renaissance and the May 21 flight to Granada.

destinations.name: Gothic Quarter
destinations.summary: Arrival-night historic core walk that keeps the first Barcelona evening compact and scenic.

destinations.name: Barcelona Cathedral
destinations.summary: Quick first-evening anchor near the hotel before dinner in the old city.

destinations.name: Picasso Museum Barcelona
destinations.summary: Strong Sunday museum anchor before the El Born and Palau de la Música stretch.

destinations.name: El Born
destinations.summary: Walkable neighborhood for museum, church, lunch, and dinner stops on the Sunday plan.

destinations.name: Santa Maria del Mar
destinations.summary: Historic church and easy landmark within the El Born walking segment.

destinations.name: Palau de la Música Catalana
destinations.summary: Afternoon architectural highlight on the Barcelona Sunday route.

destinations.name: Arc de Triomf
destinations.summary: Optional add-on after the Palau visit before returning toward dinner.

destinations.name: Parc de la Ciutadella
destinations.summary: Optional green-space extension after Palau de la Música and Arc de Triomf.

destinations.name: Casa Milà / La Pedrera
destinations.summary: First Gaudí-house anchor on the Monday architecture day.

destinations.name: Casa Batlló
destinations.summary: Second major Gaudí visit on the Monday architecture circuit.

destinations.name: Park Güell
destinations.summary: Afternoon park visit tied to the Gaudí-house day in the current source itinerary.

destinations.name: Gràcia
destinations.summary: Late-afternoon cafe and dinner neighborhood after Park Güell.

destinations.name: MNAC
destinations.summary: Tuesday museum stop placed before the fixed Sagrada Família entry.

destinations.name: Sagrada Família
destinations.summary: Fixed timed entry at 1:30 PM on Tuesday, May 19, and one of the trip’s main anchors.

destinations.name: Sant Pau Art Nouveau Site
destinations.summary: Late-afternoon follow-up near Sagrada Família on the Tuesday plan.

destinations.name: Tossa de Mar
destinations.summary: Full-day coastal excursion replacing the earlier Blanes or Sitges idea in this updated itinerary.

destinations.name: Vila Vella
destinations.summary: The walled old town and viewpoints that define the Tossa de Mar day trip.

destinations.name: Platja Gran
destinations.summary: Main beach option during the open afternoon in Tossa de Mar.

destinations.name: Es Codolar
destinations.summary: Smaller cove option on the Tossa afternoon beach-and-viewpoint loop.

destinations.name: Granada Cathedral
destinations.summary: Easy first-evening or next-morning anchor near the Granada hotel.

destinations.name: Royal Chapel
destinations.summary: Compact historical stop paired naturally with the cathedral in central Granada.

destinations.name: Alcaicería
destinations.summary: Historic market area that fits neatly into the first Granada evening and final Granada morning.

destinations.name: Plaza Bib-Rambla
destinations.summary: Central square attached to the late-afternoon Granada orientation walk.

destinations.name: Albaicín
destinations.summary: Hilltop quarter for sunset views, first-night dinner, and the most atmospheric Granada walking stretch.

destinations.name: Mirador de San Nicolás
destinations.summary: Classic sunset lookout over the Alhambra and one of the defining Granada moments.

destinations.name: Alhambra
destinations.summary: Highest-priority Granada booking, around which the full Friday visit should be built.

destinations.name: Generalife
destinations.summary: Gardens-and-palaces follow-up within the Alhambra complex.

destinations.name: Nasrid Palaces
destinations.summary: Timed-entry centerpiece within the Alhambra visit and the critical scheduling constraint for the day.

destinations.name: Monasterio de San Jerónimo
destinations.summary: Optional extra site on the final Granada morning if the city-center list is already covered.

destinations.name: Real Alcázar of Seville
destinations.summary: Top-priority Seville monument and the main Sunday morning booking anchor.

destinations.name: Santa Cruz
destinations.summary: Most walkable district in the monument core, connecting the Alcázar, Cathedral, lunch, and evening routes.

destinations.name: Patio de Banderas
destinations.summary: Key stop within the Santa Cruz walking segment after the Alcázar.

destinations.name: Murillo Gardens
destinations.summary: Shaded extension within the Santa Cruz afternoon route.

destinations.name: Seville Cathedral
destinations.summary: Sunday afternoon monument stop once the cultural-visit timing opens up.

destinations.name: Giralda
destinations.summary: Bell-tower climb paired with the Cathedral on the Sunday sightseeing route.

destinations.name: Las Setas / Metropol Parasol
destinations.summary: Sunset and dinner district for the first full Seville evening.

destinations.name: Plaza de España
destinations.summary: Open-air Monday morning anchor before the park, palace, or Triana options.

destinations.name: Parque de María Luisa
destinations.summary: Cooler park follow-up to Plaza de España on the open-air Seville day.

destinations.name: Palacio de las Dueñas
destinations.summary: Preferred Monday palace option when the afternoon leans architectural rather than riverfront.

destinations.name: Triana
destinations.summary: Riverfront neighborhood for ceramics, tapas, and flamenco on the final full day.

destinations.name: Archivo de Indias
destinations.summary: Easy final-morning exterior stop near the cathedral before heading to the airport.

destinations.name: Hospital de los Venerables
destinations.summary: Optional last cultural stop if there is enough time on the final Seville morning.

days.title: Arrival + Gothic Quarter
days.heading: Arrive Barcelona + Gothic Quarter evening
days.summary: 2:20 pm - Arrive Barcelona on UA 672 at [Barcelona-El Prat Airport](#destination-barcelona-el-prat-airport). 4:30-6:30 pm - Transfer to [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel), check in, rest. 6:30-8:30 pm - Easy walk from [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel) to [Gothic Quarter](#destination-gothic-quarter), [Barcelona Cathedral](#destination-barcelona-cathedral) exterior, Placa Reial, edge of La Rambla. Dinner: Can Culleretes for a classic old Barcelona meal, or Bodega Biarritz 1881 for casual tapas near the [Gothic Quarter](#destination-gothic-quarter).

timeline.title: Plan detail
timeline.body: 2:20 pm - Arrive Barcelona on UA 672 at [Barcelona-El Prat Airport](#destination-barcelona-el-prat-airport). 4:30-6:30 pm - Transfer to [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel), check in, rest. 6:30-8:30 pm - Easy walk from [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel) to [Gothic Quarter](#destination-gothic-quarter), [Barcelona Cathedral](#destination-barcelona-cathedral) exterior, Placa Reial, edge of La Rambla. Dinner: Can Culleretes for a classic old Barcelona meal, or Bodega Biarritz 1881 for casual tapas near the [Gothic Quarter](#destination-gothic-quarter). Keep this day easy because it is your arrival day.
timeline.meta: Keep this day easy because it is your arrival day.

days.title: Picasso + El Born
days.heading: Picasso Museum + El Born + Palau de la Música
days.summary: [Picasso Museum](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona) The [Picasso Museum](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona) is a good Sunday choice because its summer-season schedule includes Sunday opening, while it is closed every Monday. ([Museu Picasso Barcelona](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona))

timeline.title: Morning
timeline.body: [Picasso Museum](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona) The [Picasso Museum](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona) is a good Sunday choice because its summer-season schedule includes Sunday opening, while it is closed every Monday. ([Museu Picasso Barcelona](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona))

timeline.title: Late morning
timeline.body: [El Born](#destination-el-born) walk [Santa Maria del Mar](#destination-santa-maria-del-mar), Carrer de Montcada, [El Born streets](#destination-el-born).

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Llamber or El Xampanyet near [El Born](#destination-el-born). Llamber is a good modern tapas option; El Xampanyet is more old-school and lively.

timeline.title: Afternoon
timeline.body: [Palau de la Música Catalana](#destination-palau-de-la-m-sica-catalana) tour

timeline.title: Optional
timeline.body: [Arc de Triomf](#destination-arc-de-triomf) + [Parc de la Ciutadella](#destination-parc-de-la-ciutadella)

timeline.title: Dinner
timeline.body: Bacaro for Venetian/Catalan-style dining, or Bar Mut if you want a stronger wine/tapas dinner. Michelin’s Barcelona Bib Gourmand list includes places such as Bacaro, Berbena, Bardeni-Caldeni, Saó, and others, so these are good value-oriented choices to consider. (Michelin Guide (https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/catalunya/barcelona/restaurants/bib-gourmand?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

days.title: Gaudí + Park Güell
days.heading: Casa Milà + Casa Batlló + Park Güell
days.summary: [Casa Milà / La Pedrera](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera) [La Pedrera](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera) shows specific visit times during booking, so choose a morning slot. ([lapedrera.com](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera))

timeline.title: Plan detail
timeline.body: This is a good Monday plan because you avoid relying on [MNAC](#destination-mnac), which is normally closed Mondays except public holidays. ([Museu Nacional d'Art de Catalunya](#destination-mnac))

timeline.title: 9:30 am
timeline.body: [Casa Milà / La Pedrera](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera) [La Pedrera](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera) shows specific visit times during booking, so choose a morning slot. ([lapedrera.com](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera))

timeline.title: 11:30 am
timeline.body: [Casa Batlló](#destination-casa-batll) [Casa Batlló](#destination-casa-batll) is open daily, with long hours from 8:30 am to 10:30 pm and last admission at 9:30 pm, so it is flexible. ([Casa Batlló](#destination-casa-batll) (https://www.casabatllo.es/en/visit/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Vinitus near [Casa Batlló](#destination-casa-batll) for easy tapas/seafood, or El Nacional if your group wants multiple food options in one elegant food hall.

timeline.title: 2:30 or 3:00 pm
timeline.body: [Park Güell](#destination-park-g-ell) Tourist ticket bands are listed from 9:30 am to 7:30 pm, so afternoon works. ([Park Güell](#destination-park-g-ell) (https://parkguell.barcelona/en/planning-your-visit/prices-and-times?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: Late afternoon
timeline.body: [Gràcia](#destination-gr-cia) walk / café break

timeline.title: Dinner
timeline.body: Botafumeiro in [Gràcia](#destination-gr-cia) for seafood if you want a bigger dinner, or La Pepita for a more casual tapas-style meal.

days.title: MNAC + Sagrada Família
days.heading: MNAC + Sagrada Família + Sant Pau area
days.summary: [MNAC](#destination-mnac) This is the safer day for [MNAC](#destination-mnac) because May–September hours are Tuesday–Saturday 10:00 am–8:00 pm, while Mondays are closed except public holidays. ([Museu Nacional d'Art de Catalunya](#destination-mnac))

routes.label: Hotel to MNAC and Sagrada Familia

timeline.title: 10:00 am–12:00 pm
timeline.body: [MNAC](#destination-mnac) This is the safer day for [MNAC](#destination-mnac) because May–September hours are Tuesday–Saturday 10:00 am–8:00 pm, while Mondays are closed except public holidays. ([Museu Nacional d'Art de Catalunya](#destination-mnac))

timeline.title: 12:00–12:45 pm
timeline.body: Transfer toward [Sagrada Família](#destination-sagrada-fam-lia) + quick lunch Do not do a long lunch before your fixed entry.

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Bardeni-Caldeni if you can reserve and keep it efficient, or a simpler café/tapas stop near [Sagrada Família](#destination-sagrada-fam-lia). Bardeni-Caldeni is one of the Barcelona Bib Gourmand-listed restaurants. (Michelin Guide (https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/catalunya/barcelona/restaurants/bib-gourmand?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: 1:30–3:15 pm
timeline.body: [Sagrada Família](#destination-sagrada-fam-lia) In April–September, [Sagrada Família](#destination-sagrada-fam-lia)’s official hours run to 8:00 pm on weekdays, so your 1:30 pm slot is comfortably within the day. (sagradafamilia.org)

timeline.title: 3:30–5:00 pm
timeline.body: [Sant Pau Art Nouveau Site](#destination-sant-pau-art-nouveau-site) / exterior and nearby walk This pairs naturally with [Sagrada Família](#destination-sagrada-fam-lia) because it is nearby.

timeline.title: Dinner
timeline.body: Casa Amàlia or Ciudad Condal in Eixample. Ciudad Condal is popular and convenient; Casa Amàlia is a better sit-down choice.

days.title: Tossa de Mar
days.heading: Full-day Tossa de Mar
days.summary: Morning - Depart [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel) for Barcelona to [Tossa de Mar](#destination-tossa-de-mar). Late morning - [Vila Vella](#destination-vila-vella) walls, old town, viewpoints Lunch: La Cuina de Can Simon if you want a special seafood-focused meal; Michelin describes it as rooted in local fishing tradition, with fish and seafood sourced daily from the auction. Alternative: Can Sophia or Capri by Josep Pages for a less formal but still good meal.

routes.label: Tossa de Mar back to hotel

timeline.title: Plan detail
timeline.body: Morning - Depart [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel) for Barcelona to [Tossa de Mar](#destination-tossa-de-mar). Late morning - [Vila Vella](#destination-vila-vella) walls, old town, viewpoints Lunch: La Cuina de Can Simon if you want a special seafood-focused meal; Michelin describes it as rooted in local fishing tradition, with fish and seafood sourced daily from the auction. Alternative: Can Sophia or Capri by Josep Pages for a less formal but still good meal. Afternoon - Beach/cove time or boat trip Late afternoon - [Platja Gran](#destination-platja-gran), [Es Codolar](#destination-es-codolar), coffee/gelato Evening - Return to [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel) in Barcelona Dinner back in Barcelona: Keep it casual near your hotel, or do El Nacional if you want something easy after the day trip. Granada
timeline.meta: Afternoon - Beach/cove time or boat trip Late afternoon - [Platja Gran](#destination-platja-gran), [Es Codolar](#destination-es-codolar), coffee/gelato Evening - Return to [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel) in Barcelona Dinner back in Barcelona: Keep it casual near your hotel, or do El Nacional if you want something easy after the day trip. Granada

days.title: Arrival in Granada
days.heading: Arrive Granada + Cathedral area + Albaicín sunset
days.summary: [Granada Cathedral](#destination-granada-cathedral) + [Royal Chapel](#destination-royal-chapel) if timing allows [Granada Cathedral](#destination-granada-cathedral) is listed Monday–Saturday 10:00 am–6:15 pm, and the [Royal Chapel](#destination-royal-chapel) is Monday–Saturday 10:15 am–6:30 pm, so arrival timing will decide how much you can do. (Love Granada (https://www.lovegranada.com/monuments/cathedral/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: Plan detail
timeline.body: Check out from [Renaissance Barcelona Hotel](#destination-renaissance-barcelona-hotel) and transfer to [BCN airport](#destination-barcelona-el-prat-airport) for VY2012. Arrive Granada and transfer to Aurea Catedral by Eurostars Hotel Company.

timeline.title: Late afternoon
timeline.body: [Granada Cathedral](#destination-granada-cathedral) + [Royal Chapel](#destination-royal-chapel) if timing allows [Granada Cathedral](#destination-granada-cathedral) is listed Monday–Saturday 10:00 am–6:15 pm, and the [Royal Chapel](#destination-royal-chapel) is Monday–Saturday 10:15 am–6:30 pm, so arrival timing will decide how much you can do. (Love Granada (https://www.lovegranada.com/monuments/cathedral/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: Early evening
timeline.body: [Alcaicería](#destination-alcaicer-a) + [Plaza Bib-Rambla](#destination-plaza-bib-rambla) Sunset — [Albaicín](#destination-albaic-n) + [Mirador de San Nicolás](#destination-mirador-de-san-nicol-s)

timeline.title: Dinner
timeline.body: Restaurante Las Tomasas in the [Albaicín](#destination-albaic-n) if you want [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra) views; it is a memorable first-night Granada dinner. For something easier near the hotel, choose La Auténtica Carmela or Bodegas Castañeda.

days.title: Alhambra day
days.heading: Alhambra day
days.summary: [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra) + [Generalife](#destination-generalife) The key is your [Nasrid Palaces](#destination-nasrid-palaces) timed entry. The [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra) opens for daytime visits, and you should plan the entire visit around the Nasrid Palace slot printed on your ticket. (Love Granada (https://www.lovegranada.com/alhambra/opening-hours/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: Morning–early afternoon
timeline.body: [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra) + [Generalife](#destination-generalife) The key is your [Nasrid Palaces](#destination-nasrid-palaces) timed entry. The [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra) opens for daytime visits, and you should plan the entire visit around the Nasrid Palace slot printed on your ticket. (Love Granada (https://www.lovegranada.com/alhambra/opening-hours/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: 8:30/9:00 am
timeline.body: Enter [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra) complex [Nasrid Palaces](#destination-nasrid-palaces) according to ticket time Alcazaba Palace of Charles V [Generalife](#destination-generalife) gardens
timeline.meta: [Nasrid Palaces](#destination-nasrid-palaces) according to ticket time Alcazaba Palace of Charles V [Generalife](#destination-generalife) gardens

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Parador de Granada if you want a special setting near the [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra), or descend to Plaza Nueva for Bodegas Castañeda.

timeline.title: Afternoon
timeline.body: Rest, Realejo walk, or Hammam Al Ándalus

timeline.title: Dinner
timeline.body: La Esquinita de Javi for seafood/tapas, or Atelier Casa de Comidas for a more refined meal. La Esquinita de Javi is often recommended as a local seafood favorite. (The Tour Guy (https://thetourguy.com/travel-blog/spain/granada/best-restaurants-in-granada-this-year/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

days.title: Granada to Seville
days.heading: Granada morning + train to Seville
days.summary: Finish Granada center Use this for anything missed:

timeline.title: Morning
timeline.body: Finish Granada center Use this for anything missed: [Royal Chapel](#destination-royal-chapel) Cathedral [Alcaicería](#destination-alcaicer-a) [Monasterio de San Jerónimo](#destination-monasterio-de-san-jer-nimo), if you want one extra site
timeline.meta: [Royal Chapel](#destination-royal-chapel) Cathedral [Alcaicería](#destination-alcaicer-a) [Monasterio de San Jerónimo](#destination-monasterio-de-san-jer-nimo), if you want one extra site

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Los Manueles near the Cathedral / Plaza Nueva, or La Auténtica Carmela for convenience near your hotel.

timeline.title: 2:30 pm-ish
timeline.body: Pick up luggage and go to Granada train station 4:00 pm — Train to Seville 7:00 pm — Arrive Seville Transfer to Apartamentos Abreu Suites

timeline.title: Dinner
timeline.body: La Brunilda if you can reserve/queue, or Mechela Arenal for a very convenient first Seville dinner near your apartment. Seville

days.title: Alcázar + Cathedral
days.heading: Alcázar + Santa Cruz + Cathedral/Giralda + Las Setas
days.summary: [Real Alcázar](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville) The [Alcázar](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville)’s April–September schedule is daily 9:30 am–7:00 pm, so Sunday morning works well. ([Alcázar de Sevilla](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville))

timeline.title: Morning
timeline.body: [Real Alcázar](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville) The [Alcázar](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville)’s April–September schedule is daily 9:30 am–7:00 pm, so Sunday morning works well. ([Alcázar de Sevilla](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville))

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Bodega [Santa Cruz](#destination-santa-cruz) Las Columnas for casual tapas in [Santa Cruz](#destination-santa-cruz), or Bar Estrella for a more comfortable sit-down option.

timeline.title: Early afternoon
timeline.body: [Barrio Santa Cruz](#destination-santa-cruz) walk [Patio de Banderas](#destination-patio-de-banderas), Calle Agua, Plaza de Doña Elvira, [Murillo Gardens](#destination-murillo-gardens).

timeline.title: 2:30 pm or later
timeline.body: [Seville Cathedral](#destination-seville-cathedral) + [Giralda](#destination-giralda) Sunday cultural visits are the timing-sensitive part; the Cathedral’s 2026 information lists Sunday cultural-visit timing later in the day, and the official site also notes Sunday free public visit from 4:30–6:00 pm by prior reservation. (Web Oficial Catedral de Sevilla (https://www.catedraldesevilla.es/en/hours-and-rates-for-the-cultural-visit-2026/?utm_source=chatgpt.com))

timeline.title: Evening / sunset
timeline.body: [Las Setas](#destination-las-setas-metropol-parasol) / [Metropol Parasol](#destination-las-setas-metropol-parasol) Dinner: El Rinconcillo for historic tapas, or Perro Viejo near [Las Setas](#destination-las-setas-metropol-parasol) for a more modern dinner.

days.title: Plaza + Triana
days.heading: Plaza de España + María Luisa Park + palace/Triana + flamenco
days.summary: [Plaza de España](#destination-plaza-de-espa-a) + [Parque de María Luisa](#destination-parque-de-mar-a-luisa) Go early for cooler weather and better photos.

timeline.title: Morning
timeline.body: [Plaza de España](#destination-plaza-de-espa-a) + [Parque de María Luisa](#destination-parque-de-mar-a-luisa) Go early for cooler weather and better photos.

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Casa Morales near the center for classic tapas, or Bodeguita Romero for a very reliable Seville lunch.

timeline.title: Afternoon option A
timeline.body: [Palacio de las Dueñas](#destination-palacio-de-las-due-as) This is my preferred Monday palace choice because the visitor schedule is straightforward: April–September 10:00 am–7:00 pm, with last access at 6:15 pm. (Michelin Guide (https://guide.michelin.com/us/en/catalunya/barcelona/restaurants/bib-gourmand?utm_source=chatgpt.com)) Actually, for the hours source, use the official [Palacio de las Dueñas](#destination-palacio-de-las-due-as) page when booking, because hours can vary by season.

timeline.title: Afternoon option B
timeline.body: [Triana](#destination-triana) Cross the river, visit [Triana](#destination-triana) Market / ceramics shops, walk Calle Betis.

timeline.title: Dinner
timeline.body: Casa Cuesta in [Triana](#destination-triana), or Blanca Paloma for classic [Triana](#destination-triana) tapas. Flamenco: choose a show in [Triana](#destination-triana), Arenal, or [Santa Cruz](#destination-santa-cruz) depending on availability.
timeline.meta: Flamenco: choose a show in [Triana](#destination-triana), Arenal, or [Santa Cruz](#destination-santa-cruz) depending on availability.

days.title: Seville + flight back
days.heading: Easy Seville morning + fly to Barcelona
days.summary: Slow breakfast + shopping around Sierpes / Tetuán

timeline.title: Plan detail
timeline.body: Since you leave on VY2219, keep this flexible.

timeline.title: Morning options
timeline.body: Slow breakfast + shopping around Sierpes / Tetuán [Archivo de Indias](#destination-archivo-de-indias) exterior Final Cathedral/Arenal walk Short visit to [Hospital de los Venerables](#destination-hospital-de-los-venerables) if you want one last cultural stop
timeline.meta: [Archivo de Indias](#destination-archivo-de-indias) exterior Final Cathedral/Arenal walk Short visit to [Hospital de los Venerables](#destination-hospital-de-los-venerables) if you want one last cultural stop

timeline.title: Lunch
timeline.body: Tradevo Centro for a polished final lunch, or Bodeguita Romero if you did not go on May 25.

timeline.title: Airport
timeline.body: Leave central Seville about 2.5–3 hours before your flight, especially with luggage.

timeline.title: Book these first
timeline.body: [Alhambra](#destination-alhambra) + [Nasrid Palaces](#destination-nasrid-palaces) [Sagrada Família](#destination-sagrada-fam-lia), already fixed for May 19 at 1:30 pm [Real Alcázar of Seville](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville) [Seville Cathedral](#destination-seville-cathedral) + [Giralda](#destination-giralda) [Casa Batlló](#destination-casa-batll) + [Casa Milà](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera) [Park Güell](#destination-park-g-ell) [Picasso Museum](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona) Restaurants with reservations: Las Tomasas, La Cuina de Can Simón, La Brunilda/Mechela, Casa Cuesta/Blanca Paloma, Botafumeiro.
timeline.meta: [Sagrada Família](#destination-sagrada-fam-lia), already fixed for May 19 at 1:30 pm [Real Alcázar of Seville](#destination-real-alc-zar-of-seville) [Seville Cathedral](#destination-seville-cathedral) + [Giralda](#destination-giralda) [Casa Batlló](#destination-casa-batll) + [Casa Milà](#destination-casa-mil-la-pedrera) [Park Güell](#destination-park-g-ell) [Picasso Museum](#destination-picasso-museum-barcelona) Restaurants with reservations: Las Tomasas, La Cuina de Can Simón, La Brunilda/Mechela, Casa Cuesta/Blanca Paloma, Botafumeiro.
